message:
test(raptorq): stabilize corrupted-repair decode assertions with known-good seed

This commit tightens two corruption-path decoder tests so their failure signal is deterministic and attributable to the injected corruption rather than incidental parameter instability.

Changes in `src/raptorq/decoder.rs`:
- `decode_corrupted_repair_symbol_reports_corrupt_output`
  - switches seed from `2027` to `42` (aligned with established known-good decode paths),
  - adds a pre-corruption sanity decode assertion to prove the unmodified received set is solvable,
  - keeps the corruption injection and explicit `CorruptDecodedOutput` expectation unchanged.
- `decode_with_proof_corrupted_repair_symbol_reports_failure_reason`
  - switches seed from `2028` to the same known-good seed `42`.

The behavioral contract remains the same: a valid baseline decode must succeed, and a targeted repair-symbol tamper must fail with corruption-class evidence. The added baseline check narrows debugging scope when this test regresses in the future.
